name: Crossfader
description: This algorithm crossfades between two or more sets of signals (from mono up to sets of eight channels). When crossfading between more than two sets of signals, it behaves like a ‘scanning’ or ‘morphing’ mixer. Three different crossfade curves are available: Equal gain (Appropriate for crossfading phase-coherent material), Equal power (Appropriate for crossfading non-phase-coherent material), and Transition (DJ-style crossfade where both sources are at full gain at the 50% position). Inputs A to L, and the output, use a contiguous range of busses, starting with the one set via the parameters, and with the channel count set by the ‘Width’ parameter. For example, to crossfade stereo signals, set the Width to 2. The crossfade can be controlled by both a parameter and a CV, which are simply summed if both are in use.
gui_description: The display shows a graphical representation of the crossfade curves, and the amount of the two signals that are combined in the output mix.

parameters.name: Crossfader
parameters.description: The crossfade position, from 0% (input A only) to 100% (input B only).

parameters.name: Input A
parameters.description: The first bus for input A.

parameters.name: Input B
parameters.description: The first bus for input B.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The number of busses to process. For example for stereo signals, set this to 2.

parameters.name: Number of inputs
parameters.description: The number of inputs to use.

parameters.name: Input C
parameters.description: The first bus for input C.
